satisfied_constraints.description: Each location has specific time windows
Total tour must complete within time limit

schedule.description: Compile campuses locations, start/end point, and time window constraints into a structured dataset for planning.
Compute travel times between all locations (library, cafeteria, gym, student_center) and start/end (entrance) to build the routing cost matrix.
Generate and evaluate potential visit sequences using current data to seed routing optimization, considering start and end at entrance.
Build tentative schedules for each candidate sequence satisfying time windows using the travel time matrix.
Validate that proposed tours satisfy all spatial constraints and time windows; flag violations and adjust plan as needed.
Compile final tour plan (start at entrance, visit all locations within windows, return to entrance) and prepare execution briefing for deployment.

satisfied_constraints.description: Each tour must complete within time limit

schedule.description: Validate and normalize input data (guide availability, group sizes, preferred times, and time limit); produce a cleaned dataset and constraint flags for planning.
Generate initial feasible group-to-guide assignment options considering guide availability, capacity limits, and each group's preferred time; retain multiple candidate allocations for optimization.
Compute optimized schedule to minimize group wait times and balance workload across guides using the feasible assignments; output a final recommended allocation with start times for each group.
Construct a concrete execution plan with per-guide timelines and handoffs, ensuring each tour completes within the time limit and capacity constraints.
Validate the final execution plan against all constraints, and prepare a ready-to-deploy plan with contingency checks.
Feasibility check across all constraints: validate vehicle capacities, fuel, passenger arrival deadlines, and overall resource feasibility for the given fleet and requests.
Generate candidate ride assignments and routing scenarios using optimization to minimize total travel distance given current vehicles and passenger requests.
Evaluate candidate scenarios to ensure all passenger deadlines are achievable under constraints; filter feasible scenarios for further planning.
Compute passenger-vehicle assignment ensuring capacity and minimal projected distance; produce a preferred allocation across vehicles.
Create a temporal scheduling plan for each vehicle (pickup/dropoff sequence with timing) that respects deadlines and fuel constraints.
Integrate the allocations and schedules into a final executable plan; output recommended routes, timings, and passenger mappings.
Validate vehicles, passenger data, deadlines, capacities, and route constraints against the problem specification to ensure feasibility before planning.
Compute initial vehicle-to-passenger assignment and route feasibility for passenger1 considering vehicle capacity and passenger deadline.
Create a time-based schedule with pickup/dropoff times using baseline travel times and capacity constraints.
Define dynamic route availability monitoring, delay detection, and feasibility checks with triggers for disruptions.
Generate contingency routing and reallocation plans to adapt to potential disruptions and dynamic changes in route feasibility.

Develop a high-level schedule aligning guest arrivals, errands windows, ceremony deadline, and vehicle usage slots. Output: time windows per task, contingency buffers, and milestone checkpoints to enable parallel task execution.

Allocate and book transportation for arriving guests (airport 14:00, hotel 15:00) translating arrivals into church/reception transport needs. Ensure vehicle capacity and routing respect the ceremony deadline. Produce per-guest transport plan and vehicle load plans.

Plan all errands (flowers, cake, decorations, photographer, music): assign errands to vehicles, determine pickup/drop-off order, create routes and time slots that fit within the ceremony timeline. Produce a master errand route and vehicle assignment plan.

Evaluate and optimize vehicle usage against passenger and errand requirements to ensure no vehicle overload and efficient sharing. Provide alternative configurations if needed (e.g., shuttle sequences, shared rides).
Validate the complete plan against all constraints (ceremony deadline, vehicle passenger capacity, errand dependencies). Flag infeasibilities and produce a final, executable plan ready for execution.

Define the dinner deadline, total time window for meal preparation, and airport pickup windows. Establish initial high-level schedule constraints and check for obvious feasibility (dinner by deadline, utensils, kitchen capacity).

Assign cooking tasks (turkey, stuffing, gravy, potatoes, vegetables, dessert) to mom and dad based on skills, and determine plausible sequencing and oven/stove load to respect kitchen capacity and dependencies.
Plan airport pickup for dad: schedule car2 run from airport to home within arrival window, coordinate with mom's status and travel times, ensuring car capacity is not exceeded.

Create kitchen workflow with detailed sequencing, parallelizable tasks, and resource constraints to minimize conflicts (e.g., oven, burners, prep space) while respecting task dependencies and assigned personnel from S2.
Validate the full plan for feasibility, consistency with constraints, and generate a final, executable schedule with assigned resources and contingency notes.

achieved_goals.description: Maximize aid delivery to affected areas

schedule.description: Validate available resources, region needs, and emergency deadlines against constraints to establish feasibility.
Assign personnel (teams) and vehicles to regions based on identified needs, skills, and availability.
Develop concrete movement plans (routes and vehicle allocations) for each region using helicopters and trucks, considering capacity and speed.
Create time-bound delivery and response schedules aligned with deadlines and transit times.
Evaluate uncertainty scenarios and optimize allocations for robustness across evolving needs.
Validate end-to-end feasibility and generate an operational briefing for stakeholders.
Constraint feasibility verification for wedding deadline, vehicle capacities, and route availability using current resources and errands.
Initial fleet assignment: allocate vehicles (van, car1) to guest transport and errands respecting capacities and starting locations.
Draft a synchronized timeline for guest arrivals and errands, incorporating vehicle availability, locations, and the ceremony deadline.
Plan feasible routes for each task (guest pickups, errands, and transitions) considering current road closures and travel times.
Define real-time replanning protocol to adapt schedules and routes in response to disruptions (closures, delays, or capacity changes).
Generate integrative plan communication and task handoffs to guests and staff, including contingency options and alerts.

Assess flight arrival windows for mom and dad, identify dinner deadline, kitchen workspace capacity, and available resources (vehicles, cooking_tasks). Produce initial feasible time windows and constraints to guide scheduling.

Assign cooking responsibilities to mom and dad based on their skills (mom: turkey; dad: potatoes) and allocate remaining tasks (stuffing, gravy, potatoes) to capable family members or placeholders, ensuring alignment with flight schedules.

Create a detailed, parallelizable cooking timeline for turkey, stuffing, gravy, and potatoes aligned with kitchen capacity, overlap constraints, and recovery windows for flight delays. Include start/finish times and required utensils/workspaces.
Plan vehicle movements for airport pickups/drop-offs and kitchen supply transfers, respecting car capacities and locations, ensuring arrival/departure windows align with flight schedules.
Integrate cooking schedule with dinner serving deadline, implement recovery windows for potential flight delays, and validate feasibility against all constraints.

Define project objectives, constraints, and evaluation metrics. Produce a constraint/cost-risk model and baseline data interpretation to guide all later planning (budget limits, deadlines, supplier capacities, lead times, infrastructure dependencies).

Develop procurement strategy: allocate components to suppliers with respect to lead times, capacities, and costs; generate a detailed procurement plan per component (gpu_chips, memory, cooling, power_supply, pcb).

Plan manufacturing and assembly: allocate components to facilities (assembly, testing), verify capacity constraints, and create a production/assembly schedule including lead times and handoffs between stages.
Inventory optimization: determine optimal reorder points and safety stocks for components across suppliers and facilities to minimize holding costs while meeting demand.
Integrated delivery planning: schedule component deliveries to meet deadlines for assembly/testing, identify critical paths, and align with facility capacities.
Risk assessment and mitigation planning: quantify supply chain risks, evaluate tradeoffs, and propose contingency actions (alternative suppliers, buffer stocks, schedule slack).
Consolidated execution plan and validation: integrate all outputs, produce the final plan document, and perform feasibility validation against constraints.

satisfied_constraints.description: Each location has specific time windows
Total tour must complete within time limit

schedule.description: Aggregate problem data: confirm locations, start/end points (entrance), time windows for each location, and inter-location travel times; establish total time limit.
Generate candidate visit sequences that cover all locations and return to start, producing feasible route options that respect spatial constraints.
Develop a time-window compliant schedule for each candidate route, mapping sequence to actual time stamps and ensuring end time within limit.
Verify all constraints for selected route(s): time windows, total duration, and start/end points; report feasibility and any violations.

Create a coordination and resource allocation plan: assign feasible tasks to agent capabilities (logistics, scheduler, data_analyst, validator) and outline parallel execution with minimal dependencies.
Produce final integrated plan and a concise execution guide for agents, including contingencies for delays and replan triggers.

Generate initial parallel assignment proposals mapping groups to guides that satisfy feasibility, producing multiple candidate allocations to maximize parallelism.
Run optimization to minimize wait times and balance workload across guides given candidate allocations; output recommended plan.
Validate the recommended plan against all constraints (availability, capacity, time limits) and flag any infeasibilities; provide fallback adjustments if needed.
Produce the final, executable execution plan with timelines, guide assignments, and handoffs; prepare operational directives for guides and coordinators.
